title: Concepción (Chile)

infobox.key: • [City](/wiki/List_of_cities_in_Chile "List of cities in Chile") and [Commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile")

infobox.value: (Spanish pronunciation: [[konseβˈsjon]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Es-Concepci%C3%B3n.ogg "File:Es-Concepción.ogg"); originally: *Concepción de la Madre Santísima de la Luz*, "Conception of the Blessed Mother of Light") is a city and [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile") in south-central [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"), and the geographical and demographic core of the [Greater Concepción](/wiki/Greater_Concepci%C3%B3n "Greater Concepción") [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area"), it is the second largest city in Chile by urban area and one of the three major conurbations in the country.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It has a significant impact on domestic trade[[5]](#cite_note-5) being part of the most heavily industrialized region in the country.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It is the seat of the [Concepción Province](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n_Province,_Chile "Concepción Province, Chile") and the capital of the [Biobío Region](/wiki/Biob%C3%ADo_Region "Biobío Region"). It sits about 500 km south of the nation's capital, [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago").

**Concepción** (Spanish pronunciation: [[konseβˈsjon]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Es-Concepci%C3%B3n.ogg "File:Es-Concepción.ogg"); originally: *Concepción de la Madre Santísima de la Luz*, "Conception of the Blessed Mother of Light") is a city and [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile") in south-central [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"), and the geographical and demographic core of the [Greater Concepción](/wiki/Greater_Concepci%C3%B3n "Greater Concepción") [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area"), it is the second largest city in Chile by urban area and one of the three major conurbations in the country.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It has a significant impact on domestic trade[[5]](#cite_note-5) being part of the most heavily industrialized region in the country.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It is the seat of the [Concepción Province](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n_Province,_Chile "Concepción Province, Chile") and the capital of the [Biobío Region](/wiki/Biob%C3%ADo_Region "Biobío Region"). It sits about 500 km south of the nation's capital, [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago").

The city was first settled in the [Bay of Concepción](/wiki/Bay_of_Concepci%C3%B3n "Bay of Concepción"), in the zone that would later become the commune of [Penco](/wiki/Penco "Penco"), now part of the [Concepción conurbation](/wiki/Greater_Concepci%C3%B3n "Greater Concepción"). The city's demonym, *[penquista](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/penquista "wikt:penquista")*, comes from the place of its original foundation. The city center and historic district is located in the [Valle de la Mocha](/wiki/Valle_de_la_Mocha "Valle de la Mocha") (La Mocha Valley), where it relocated after serious damage left by [an earthquake](/wiki/1751_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1751 Concepción earthquake") in 1751.

The origin of Concepción dates back to 1550, when it was founded by Pedro de Valdivia as part of the [Spanish Empire](/wiki/Spanish_Empire "Spanish Empire"), under the name of *Concepción de María Purísima del Nuevo Extremo*, and was the capital of the [Kingdom of Chile](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Chile "Kingdom of Chile") between 1565 and 1573, retaining the unofficial position of military capital[[7]](#cite_note-encicloreg2006-7) for the rest of the [colonial period](/wiki/Colonial_Chile "Colonial Chile"). The city was an important site in the [struggle for Chile's independence](/wiki/Independence_of_Chile "Independence of Chile"), with the Chilean declaration of Independence being held at Concepción's *Plaza de la Independencia*. Until the election of [Manuel Montt](/wiki/Manuel_Montt "Manuel Montt") in 1851 as president, executive power in independent Chile was dominated by Concepción elites.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The city is a known [college town](/wiki/College_town "College town") in Chile,[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) as it is home to numerous educational institutions, including the [University of Concepción](/wiki/University_of_Concepci%C3%B3n "University of Concepción"), the [University of the Bío Bío](/wiki/University_of_the_B%C3%ADo_B%C3%ADo "University of the Bío Bío"), and the [Catholic University of the Most Holy Conception](/wiki/Catholic_University_of_the_Most_Holy_Conception "Catholic University of the Most Holy Conception"). The commune also contains various historical bridges, murals, parks and lakes, as well as important cultural venues such as the *Teatro Biobío*, the *[Casa del Arte](/wiki/Casa_del_Arte "Casa del Arte")*, the local Natural History Museum, and the *Teatro Universidad de Concepción*.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Concepción was founded by Don [Pedro de Valdivia](/wiki/Pedro_de_Valdivia "Pedro de Valdivia")[[11]](#cite_note-11) in 1550 north of the [Bío Bío River](/wiki/B%C3%ADo_B%C3%ADo_River "Bío Bío River"), at the site which is today known as [Penco](/wiki/Penco "Penco"). At that time it was given the name *Concepción de María Purísima del Nuevo Extremo* (Mary Immaculate Conception of the New End). The new settlement of Concepción was just a few kilometers north of *[La Frontera](/wiki/La_Frontera_(geographical_region) "La Frontera (geographical region)")* (The Frontier), the boundary between Spanish territory and the land of the [Mapuche](/wiki/Mapuche "Mapuche"), an American Indian ethnic group that remained independent until the 1870s. The settlement was formally recognized by the Spanish authorities as a town two years later by a royal decree. It was given a coat-of-arms that is still in use today.

At the time of the Spanish arrival to the Concepción area chronicler [Jerónimo de Vivar](/wiki/Jer%C3%B3nimo_de_Vivar "Jerónimo de Vivar") noted local Mapuches wore gold and silver [bracelets](/wiki/Bracelet "Bracelet") and "sort of crowns". This is interpreted either as [Incan](/wiki/Incas_in_Central_Chile "Incas in Central Chile") gifts, war spoils from defeated Incas, or the adoption of Incan [metallurgy](/wiki/Metallurgy "Metallurgy").[[12]](#cite_note-Silva1983-12)

Although Concepción was a significant military settlement for the [Captaincy General of Chile](/wiki/Captaincy_General_of_Chile "Captaincy General of Chile"), it was overrun and destroyed by [Mapuche armies](/wiki/Arauco_War "Arauco War") in 1554, and once again after being refounded in 1555. Concepción was restored during the governorship of [García Hurtado de Mendoza](/wiki/Garc%C3%ADa_Hurtado_de_Mendoza,_5th_Marquis_of_Ca%C3%B1ete "García Hurtado de Mendoza, 5th Marquis of Cañete") when he landed there and built a fort on the Alto de Pinto in 1557. The town was refounded once more on January 6, 1558, by captain Jerónimo de Villegas. It became the headquarters of the military forces engaged against the [Mapuche](/wiki/Mapuche "Mapuche") in [La Araucanía](/wiki/Araucan%C3%ADa_(historic_region) "Araucanía (historic region)") over the next two centuries, growing to a population of 10,000 despite a [siege](/wiki/Siege "Siege") in 1564 and other attacks by the Mapuche. Concepción was the home of the [Real Audiencia](/wiki/Audiencia_Real "Audiencia Real") from 1565 to 1575.

[Earthquakes](/wiki/Earthquakes "Earthquakes") and [tsunamis](/wiki/Tsunamis "Tsunamis"), which razed the town in [1570](/wiki/1570_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1570 Concepción earthquake"), 1657, 1687, 1730 and 1751, led the authorities to move the town to its current site in the [Valle de la Mocha](/wiki/Valle_de_la_Mocha "Valle de la Mocha"), alongside the [Bío Bío River](/wiki/B%C3%ADo_B%C3%ADo_River "Bío Bío River"); the old site lay empty until March 29, 1842, when the present town of [Penco](/wiki/Penco "Penco") was founded.

The new site for the town of Concepción became the main town of the Intendancy of Concepción, whose jurisdiction extended from the [Maule River](/wiki/Maule_River "Maule River") to La Frontera. The first Intendant of Concepción was the Irishman [Ambrose O'Higgins, Marquis of Osorno](/wiki/Ambrose_O%27Higgins,_Marquis_of_Osorno "Ambrose O'Higgins, Marquis of Osorno"), who later became [Royal Governor of Chile](/wiki/Royal_Governor_of_Chile "Royal Governor of Chile") and [Viceroy of Peru](/wiki/List_of_Viceroys_of_Peru "List of Viceroys of Peru").

When the First National Government Board met in [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") on September 18, 1810, citizens of Concepción joined up. Concepción was used as the point of entry by the Spanish Army in the attempt by the [Viceroyalty of Peru](/wiki/Viceroyalty_of_Peru "Viceroyalty of Peru") to re-conquer Chile. Concepción politicians and soldiers became a significant political force in the newly independent country.

On January 1, 1818, Ambrose O'Higgins's son, [Bernardo O'Higgins](/wiki/Bernardo_O%27Higgins "Bernardo O'Higgins"), proclaimed and took the oath of the [Chilean War of Independence](/wiki/Chilean_War_of_Independence "Chilean War of Independence") in the main square of Concepción, which since then has been known as "Plaza de la Independencia". Until the moment of independence, the city had been the scene of [various engagements](/wiki/Battle_of_Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile_(disambiguation) "Battle of Concepción, Chile (disambiguation)") in its old and new settlements.

On February 20, 1835, the town again was largely destroyed by [an earthquake](/wiki/1835_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1835 Concepción earthquake") and had to be rebuilt.

By 1875, a key British community and German colony existed within the province. By 1895, there was an even larger Spanish presence.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The [Universidad de Concepción](/wiki/Universidad_de_Concepci%C3%B3n "Universidad de Concepción"), founded in 1919, became the first secular private university in Chile. The neighboring harbor of Talcahuano is the site of the largest naval base in Chile.

On February 27, 2010, an [8.8 magnitude earthquake](/wiki/2010_Chile_earthquake "2010 Chile earthquake") struck the city of Concepción, killing more than 521 people and injuring thousands nationwide. Following the earthquake, geologists relying on global positioning satellite (GPS) data concluded that the city had been displaced roughly 3 meters (9.8 feet) to the west as a result of the event.[[14]](#cite_note-14) The tsunami that followed missed the city.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

After the [2010 Chile earthquake](/wiki/2010_Chile_earthquake "2010 Chile earthquake"), a [prison riot](/wiki/Prison_riot "Prison riot") began in Concepción's [El Manzano](/wiki/El_Manzano_(prison) "El Manzano (prison)") prison following a failed escape attempt by the inmates. Different parts of the prison were set on fire and the riot was controlled only after the guards shot into the air and received help from military units.[[16]](#cite_note-16) As of March 5, 2010, a Peruvian field hospital has been deployed to the city.[[17]](#cite_note-UKPA-2010-03-06-17)

## Seismology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Seismology")]

Like most of the Chilean territory, the Concepción region is [seismically active](/wiki/Seismology "Seismology"), with much of Concepción destroyed by a violent [earthquake in 1939](/wiki/1939_Chill%C3%A1n_earthquake "1939 Chillán earthquake"). An [earthquake in 1953](/wiki/1953_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1953 Concepción earthquake") damaged 15% of the town's buildings. Another major [earthquake in 1960](/wiki/1960_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1960 Concepción earthquake") had a nearby epicenter.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

On February 27, 2010, [a magnitude 8.8 earthquake](/wiki/2010_Chile_earthquake "2010 Chile earthquake") struck at 35.846°S, 72.719°W, 115 km (71 mi) NNE of the city. The United States Geological Survey said the earthquake struck at 0334 GMT on Saturday at a depth of 35 km (22 mi). The effects were felt as far away as [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"), Brazil—4,620 kilometres (2,870 mi) away.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Education")]

Concepción is known as "the university city"[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20) thanks to the numerous universities within the urban agglomeration,[[21]](#cite_note-21) such as the [Universidad de Concepción](/wiki/Universidad_de_Concepci%C3%B3n "Universidad de Concepción"), [Universidad Católica de la Santísima Concepción](/wiki/Universidad_Cat%C3%B3lica_de_la_Sant%C3%ADsima_Concepci%C3%B3n "Universidad Católica de la Santísima Concepción") and [Universidad del Biobío](/wiki/Universidad_del_Biob%C3%ADo "Universidad del Biobío"). This city also has numerous headquarters for many other universities. In addition, this metropolis has a large educational offer, focused on institutes, centers of technical formation and the universities aforementioned.

### Universities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Universities")]

The University Pencopolitana whose name was **Pontificia Universidad Pencopolitana de La Concepción** (Pontifical Pencopolitana University of The Conception) was an old university founded through a decree of the Bishop of Concepción, delivered to the administration of the [Society of Jesus](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") which functioned between the years 1724 and 1767. On May 24, 1751, an [earthquake](/wiki/1751_Concepci%C3%B3n_earthquake "1751 Concepción earthquake") followed by a [tsunami](/wiki/Tsunami "Tsunami"), ravaged the city causing serious damage, including the destruction of the library of the university and all of its funds. As a product of the fury of the waters, a rich collection of texts of the governance of southern Chile were lost, which resulted in a disaster for the culture and collective memory of the city. Due to the natural disasters, it was decided to relocate the city to the called [Valle de la Mocha](/wiki/Valle_de_la_Mocha "Valle de la Mocha"), location where it is currently situated. Slowly the seminary was revived in the new location, in a period of decline in academic activity in Chilean monastic universities after the creation of the [Universidad de San Felipe](/wiki/Universidad_de_San_Felipe "Universidad de San Felipe"), yet some degrees continued to be granted before the [Suppression of the Society of Jesus](/wiki/Suppression_of_the_Society_of_Jesus "Suppression of the Society of Jesus") in 1767.
The aforementioned University is considered to be the natural and legitimate predecessor of the [Universidad Católica de la Santísima Concepción](/wiki/Universidad_Cat%C3%B3lica_de_la_Sant%C3%ADsima_Concepci%C3%B3n "Universidad Católica de la Santísima Concepción") (UCSC), erected by the Archbishop of Concepción in 1991, from the [Talcahuano](/wiki/Talcahuano "Talcahuano") Regional Headquarters of the [Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile](/wiki/Pontificia_Universidad_Cat%C3%B3lica_de_Chile "Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile").

The image of the Pontifical University of Concepción Pencopolitana, is still valid, for His Majesty [King Juan Carlos I of Spain](/wiki/Juan_Carlos_I_of_Spain "Juan Carlos I of Spain") in his 1990 speech on the occasion of his inauguration as Doctor Honoris Causa in the [University of Chile](/wiki/University_of_Chile "University of Chile"), in Santiago de Chile, said: "The work of the Pontifical University Pencopolitana should not be ignored, even before the creation of the University of San Felipe" (Juan Carlos's account fails to acknowledge the existence of San Felipe's predecessor, the Dominican Universidad de Santo Tomás de Aquino, from the sixteenth century in Santiago).

Meanwhile, the University of Chile allowed schools in the city to teach courses in law which allowed men to obtain a law degree. Among its students was Henry Urrutia Manzano, who decades later became president of the Supreme Court of Chile.

The [Universidad de Concepción](/wiki/Universidad_de_Concepci%C3%B3n "Universidad de Concepción") was created in 1919 by a group of citizens including [Enrique Molina Garmendia](/wiki/Enrique_Molina_Garmendia "Enrique Molina Garmendia"), its first rector.

Eventually, the university began to receive state support, becoming part of the traditional universities, becoming one of the three most important universities in the country, and the most important at the regional level.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Transportation")]

The city is served by [Carriel Sur International Airport](/wiki/Carriel_Sur_International_Airport "Carriel Sur International Airport") in Talcahuano. The metro area's public transportation comprises a number of bus lines that run through several avenues and streets of the communes belonging to the agglomeration, as well as the [commuter rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail "Commuter rail") system known as [Biotrén](/wiki/Biotr%C3%A9n "Biotrén").

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Climate")]

The climate of Concepción is classified as [Köppen:](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *[Csb](/wiki/Warm-summer_Mediterranean_climate "Warm-summer Mediterranean climate"), [warm summer mediterranean](/wiki/Warm-summer_Mediterranean "Warm-summer Mediterranean")*, with a dry mild summer. The cool waters of the Pacific Ocean help to maintain mild temperatures throughout the year. Temperatures rarely exceed 30 °C (86 °F) or fall below 0 °C (32.0 °F). In the six-month period between May and October, the city receives approximately 83% of its total annual precipitation, which totals 984 mm (38.7 in). The wettest month since records began in 1912 was June 2000 with 565.7 millimetres (22.3 in), whilst all months from November to April have been rainless on occasions.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

According to data collected in 2024 in the Census of the [National Institute of Statistics](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Statistics_(Chile) "National Institute of Statistics (Chile)"), the township has an area of 221.6 km2 (86 sq mi) and a population of 230,375 inhabitants, of whom 110,985 are men and 119,390 are women.

As a commune, Concepcion is home to 14.28% of the total population of the region. 1.88% (4,058 persons) is for the rural population and 98.12% (212,003) for the urban population. Moreover, 5.5% of the country's population lives in the Greater Concepción metro area.

Between 1970 and 1982 there is a large increase in the population. One reason is the inclusion of [San Pedro](/wiki/San_Pedro_de_la_Paz "San Pedro de la Paz"), segregated from the municipality of [Coronel](/wiki/Coronel,_Chile "Coronel, Chile").

The Concepción commune population sharply declined in 1996 as its territory was divided, creating [Chiguayante](/wiki/Chiguayante "Chiguayante") and [San Pedro de la Paz](/wiki/San_Pedro_de_la_Paz "San Pedro de la Paz"). The number of people living in these communes has grown quickly since they have become [bedroom communities](/wiki/Bedroom_communities "Bedroom communities") of *Concepción*, i.e. residential cities that do not have a center with many shops, as is the case of Concepcion.

There is a significant percentage of foreign residents in the city, the most numerous are the Spanish, Italian and US expatriate communities. There are also smaller segments of German, French, British, [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_people "Dutch people"), [Greek](/wiki/Greek_people "Greek people"), [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_people "Portuguese people"), [Croatian](/wiki/Croats "Croats"), Scandinavian, [Arab](/wiki/Arab "Arab") and Australian descendants in a city settled by waves of immigration.

## Cultural life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Cultural life")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/A%C3%B1o_nuevo_en_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg/250px-A%C3%B1o_nuevo_en_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg)](/wiki/File:A%C3%B1o_nuevo_en_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg)

Fireworks in celebration for the arrival of 2007 in Concepción

To an outsider, there may be confusion between the [demonyms](/wiki/Demonym "Demonym") of the inhabitants of Concepción and the nearby city of Penco. Due to the previous location of Concepción being where Penco currently stands, inhabitants of Concepción are called *penquistas* while inhabitants of Penco are known as *pencones*. Additionally, the unofficial term *pencopolitano* has been coined to refer to locals of any commune belonging to the [metropolis](/wiki/Greater_Concepci%C3%B3n "Greater Concepción") or, in a wider definition, to the [Concepción province](/wiki/Concepcion_Province,_Chile "Concepcion Province, Chile"), although the term penquista can technically be used in this case as well.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/REC_2020.jpg/250px-REC_2020.jpg)](/wiki/File:REC_2020.jpg)

*Rock en Conce* festival, 2020.

In 2014, the *Rock en Conce* festival began to take shape. It would be held outdoors, in the city's *Parque Bicentenario* (Bicentennial Park), and access to it would be free. The aim of the festival would be to restore the musical spotlight that the city embodied and that earned it the reputation of being the *cradle of Chilean rock* and, at the same time, to establish itself as a major tourist attraction for the city.[[34]](#cite_note-34) Its first edition took place on Saturday, March 7, 2015, and since then it has been held with great success every year at the end of each summer, bringing together emerging local bands with major and renowned artists from the national and international scene.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/Lostres.jpg/250px-Lostres.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lostres.jpg)

The [Los Tres](/wiki/Los_Tres "Los Tres") band was born in the city of Concepción

### Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Music")]

Concepción has been described as the "Chilean capital of rock",[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37) since numerous bands of this [genre](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") have started their careers in the city, such as [Los Tres](/wiki/Los_Tres "Los Tres"), [Los Bunkers](/wiki/Los_Bunkers "Los Bunkers"), Emociones Clandestinas, Santos Dumont, [De Saloon](/wiki/De_Saloon "De Saloon"), Machuca and Julius Popper, among others. Numerous bands have played their first major concerts in the city, such as [Los Prisioneros](/wiki/Los_Prisioneros "Los Prisioneros").[[38]](#cite_note-38)

The city's music venues are a main feature of the [tourist route](/wiki/Scenic_route "Scenic route"), *La Ruta de la Música*, a project driven by the National Service of Tourism (SERNATUR), where besides the rock scene, the local jazz and folk scenes are also shown.[[39]](#cite_note-39)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Sports")]

Concepción was one of the host cities of the official [1959 Basketball World Cup](/wiki/1959_Basketball_World_Cup "1959 Basketball World Cup"), where [Chile](/wiki/Chile_national_basketball_team "Chile national basketball team") won the bronze medal.

Today, Concepción is home to three professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams:

* [Club Deportivo Universidad de Concepción](/wiki/Club_Deportivo_Universidad_de_Concepci%C3%B3n "Club Deportivo Universidad de Concepción"), currently in the [Primera División](/wiki/Chilean_Primera_Divisi%C3%B3n "Chilean Primera División")
* [Club Deportivo Ferroviario Almirante Arturo Fernández Vial](/wiki/Club_Deportivo_Ferroviario_Almirante_Arturo_Fern%C3%A1ndez_Vial "Club Deportivo Ferroviario Almirante Arturo Fernández Vial"), currently, in the Chilean second division
* [Club de Deportes Concepción](/wiki/Deportes_Concepci%C3%B3n_(Chile) "Deportes Concepción (Chile)"), currently in the Chilean third division.

The most notable professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team is:

* [C.D. Universidad de Concepción](/wiki/C.D._Universidad_de_Concepci%C3%B3n_B%C3%A1squetbol "C.D. Universidad de Concepción Básquetbol"), currently in the [Liga Nacional de Básquetbol de Chile](/wiki/Liga_Nacional_de_B%C3%A1squetbol_de_Chile "Liga Nacional de Básquetbol de Chile").

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/Mercado_Central_de_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg/250px-Mercado_Central_de_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mercado_Central_de_Concepci%C3%B3n.jpg)

Concepción is the second commercial nucleus of Chile. In the image, the Concepción Central Market

Historically characterized by a strong manufacturing industry, Concepción has also been a major center for distribution and services and the financial basis of the regional economy.

### Commerce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Commerce")]

Trade in the city is concentrated in the *Plaza Independencia* (Independence Square), the pedestrian street *Alonso de Ercilla y Zúñiga* (built in 1981), and along the major avenues. One such avenue is the *Diego Barros Arana* street which has developed into the commercial center of the city and the region was until 1907 even known as "*calle Comercio*" ("Commerce Street"). New commercial centers are being developed around the old central station and the new civic district as a part of the Chilean bicentennial (September 18, 2010). A considerable percentage of local trade is taking place in settlements around the city, in communes such as [Hualpén](/wiki/Hualp%C3%A9n "Hualpén"), [Talcahuano](/wiki/Talcahuano "Talcahuano") and [San Pedro de la Paz](/wiki/San_Pedro_de_la_Paz "San Pedro de la Paz"), where there are bustling shopping centers, such as [Mall Plaza del Trébol](/w/index.php?title=Mall_Plaza_del_Tr%C3%A9bol&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mall Plaza del Trébol (page does not exist)"), and where there is a constant commercial development.

Other commercial spots, related to food, are the [Vega Monumental](/w/index.php?title=Vega_Monumental&action=edit&redlink=1 "Vega Monumental (page does not exist)") and [Mercado Central de Concepción](/wiki/Central_market_of_Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile "Central market of Concepción, Chile") (Central Market of Concepción) places offering various services and agricultural and livestock products.

## International Relations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: International Relations")]

The city of Concepción hosts several international relations institutions, such as the Regional Unit of International Affairs (URAI) of the [Regional Government of Biobío](/w/index.php?title=Regional_Government_of_Biob%C3%ADo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Regional Government of Biobío (page does not exist)"), responsible for analyzing and managing the region's bilateral and multilateral relations with [Latin America](/wiki/Latin_America "Latin America") and the rest of the world; the International Relations Commission of the Biobío Regional Council; and the regional office of the [General Directorate for Export Promotion](/w/index.php?title=General_Directorate_for_Export_Promotion&action=edit&redlink=1 "General Directorate for Export Promotion (page does not exist)") (ProChile).

### Migration Management

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Migration Management")]

In the area of international relations and migration management, the main actors in Concepción are the regional office of the [National Migration Service](/w/index.php?title=National_Migration_Service&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Migration Service (page does not exist)"), the Migration and International Police Department of the [Investigations Police of Chile](/wiki/Investigations_Police_of_Chile "Investigations Police of Chile"), and the Migrant Office of the Municipality of Concepción.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

### Internationalization in Higher Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Internationalization in Higher Education")]

In the field of international relations and higher education, the main actors in Concepción include the Directorate of International Relations[[41]](#cite_note-41) and the Center for European Studies (CEE)[[42]](#cite_note-42) of the [University of Concepción](/wiki/University_of_Concepci%C3%B3n "University of Concepción"), the General Directorate of Institutional Relations of the [University of Bío-Bío](/wiki/University_of_B%C3%ADo-B%C3%ADo "University of Bío-Bío"),[[43]](#cite_note-43) the Directorate of International Relations of the Concepción campus of the [University of Development](/wiki/University_of_Development "University of Development"),[[44]](#cite_note-44) and both the International Relations Unit of the Directorate of Outreach and Institutional Relations[[45]](#cite_note-45) and the Asia-Pacific Studies and Development Center (CEDAP)[[46]](#cite_note-46) of the [Catholic University of the Most Holy Conception](/wiki/Catholic_University_of_the_Most_Holy_Conception "Catholic University of the Most Holy Conception").

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Administration")]

As a [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile"), Concepción is a third-level [administrative division of Chile](/wiki/Administrative_division_of_Chile "Administrative division of Chile") administered by a [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council "Municipal council"), headed by an [alcalde](/wiki/Alcalde "Alcalde") who is directly elected every four years. The 2016–2020 mayor was Álvaro Ortiz Vera ([Christian Democratic Party](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_Party_(Chile) "Christian Democratic Party (Chile)")), who was first elected in 2012. The communal council has 10 seats, which are currently occupied (2016–2020) by three for [Christian Democratic Party](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_Party_(Chile) "Christian Democratic Party (Chile)"), two for [National Renewal](/wiki/National_Renewal_(Chile) "National Renewal (Chile)"), two for [Independent Democrat Union](/wiki/Independent_Democrat_Union "Independent Democrat Union"), one for [Communist Party](/wiki/Communist_Party_of_Chile "Communist Party of Chile"), one for [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_of_Chile "Socialist Party of Chile"), and one for [Radical Party](/wiki/Social_Democrat_Radical_Party "Social Democrat Radical Party"). The following members are:[[1]](#cite_note-Official-1)

| Seats | Councillor | Party |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Fabiola Troncoso Alvarado | Christian Democratic |
| 2 | Yanina Contreras Álvarez | National Renewal |
| 3 | Jaime Monjes Farias | Christian Democratic |
| 4 | Emilio Armstrong Delpin | I.D.U. |
| 5 | Christian Paulsen Espejo-Pando | I.D.U. |
| 6 | Joaquin Eguiluz Herrera | National Renewal |
| 7 | Alex Iturra Jara | Communist |
| 8 | Boris Negrete Canales | Christian Democratic |
| 9 | Patricia Garcia Mora | Socialist |
| 10 | Ricardo Trostel Provoste | Radical |

Within the [electoral divisions of Chile](/wiki/Electoral_divisions_of_Chile "Electoral divisions of Chile"), Concepción is represented in the [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_of_Chile "Chamber of Deputies of Chile") by Gaston Saavedra ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_of_Chile "Socialist Party of Chile")), José Miguel Ortiz ([PDC](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_Party "Christian Democratic Party")), Jaime Toha (PS), Felix Gonzalez ([PEV](/wiki/Green_Ecologist_Party_(Chile) "Green Ecologist Party (Chile)")), [Enrique Van Rysselberghe](/wiki/Enrique_van_Rysselberghe_Herrera "Enrique van Rysselberghe Herrera") (UDI), Sergio Bobadilla (UDI), [Francesca Muñoz](/wiki/Francesca_Mu%C3%B1oz "Francesca Muñoz") (RN) and Leonidas Romero (RN), as part of the 21st electoral district ([Concepción Province](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n_Province,_Chile "Concepción Province, Chile") without [Lota](/wiki/Lota,_Chile "Lota, Chile")). The commune is represented in the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_of_Chile "Senate of Chile") by Alejandro Navarro Brain ([MAS](/wiki/Broad_Social_Movement "Broad Social Movement")) and Jacqueline Van Rysselberghe (UDI) as part of the 12th senatorial constituency (Biobío-Costa).

## Notable events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Notable events")]

A 23 year old [Chilean](/wiki/Chileans "Chileans") forestry student named [Jorge Matute](/wiki/Lists_of_solved_missing_person_cases#1990s "Lists of solved missing person cases") disappeared mysteriously in a discotheque that was located close to Concepción on 20 November 1999. In February 2004 on a road in [Santa Juana](/wiki/Santa_Juana "Santa Juana"), Matute's remains were found and identified.[[47]](#cite_note-47)
